name: Mirador del Acebal y La Turruca – trail running por la Reserva Natural del Acebal de Garagüeta
region_intro: Jogging around Almarza offers diverse landscapes for runners, characterized by rolling hills, dense forests, and river valleys. The region provides varied terrain, including both challenging inclines and softer paths, suitable for different running preferences. This natural setting in the Soria province features an extensive network of trails, making it a suitable location for outdoor activity. The area's physical features contribute to a tranquil environment for running. Best jogging routes around Almarza The most popular jogging route is Mirador del Acebal y La Turruca – trail running por la Reserva Natural del Acebal de Garagüeta, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail that takes about 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through the Reserva Natural del Acebal de Garagüeta, offering views of the holly forest. Another top favourite among local runners is Castro Alto de la Cruz Loop, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.6 km) path. This route features varied terrain with an elevation gain of over 250 meters. Local runners also love the Puente del Canto loop from El Torilejo, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail leading through river valleys and gentle ascents, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Jogging around Almarza is defined by rolling hills, dense forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Almarza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 170 runners have used komoot to explore Almarza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Almarza?
A: There are over 15 running routes documented around Almarza, offering a variety of experiences for different preferences and fitness levels. These include a mix of moderate and challenging trails through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Almarza?
A: The running trails around Almarza feature diverse terrain, including rolling hills, dense forests, and scenic river valleys. You'll find both challenging inclines and softer paths, making it suitable for various running styles and preferences. The region is known for its tranquil environment and natural beauty.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier jog?
A: While most routes are classified as moderate, the region's varied terrain means some paths offer gentler ascents and softer surfaces. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40014075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Canto loop from El Torilejo&lt;/a&gt; features river valleys and gentle ascents, making it a good option for a less strenuous run.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might encounter on a run?
A: Many routes offer views of Almarza's natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064111784/mirador-del-acebal-y-la-turruca-trail-running-por-la-reserva-natural-del-acebal-de-garagueeta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Acebal y La Turruca trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, winds through Spain's largest holly forest, the Reserva Natural del Acebal de Garagüeta. You might also encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4000052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada Honda Rock Art Site&lt;/a&gt; or natural springs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3992864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de la Gallina Spring&lt;/a&gt; near some trails.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Almarza area?
A: Yes, several running routes around Almarza are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064115251/castro-alto-de-la-cruz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Alto de la Cruz Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 8.6 km path with varied terrain.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Almarza?
A: The running routes in Almarza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Runners often praise the tranquil environment, the diverse landscapes of rolling hills and dense forests, and the opportunity to explore the pristine countryside.

Q: Can I find any shelters or huts along the running trails?
A: Yes, some trails in the Almarza area feature huts or shelters. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064111784/mirador-del-acebal-y-la-turruca-trail-running-por-la-reserva-natural-del-acebal-de-garagueeta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Acebal y La Turruca trail&lt;/a&gt; passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5587268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Turruca Stone Hut&lt;/a&gt;. Other facilities like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3956862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valonsadero Cabin&lt;/a&gt; are also present in the wider region.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging running routes for experienced trail runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced trail runners seeking a challenge, Almarza offers several difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garray – Stone Bridge of Soria loop from Garray&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 17 km trail with significant elevation changes, perfect for pushing your limits.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Almarza?
A: Almarza offers beautiful running conditions throughout the year, with each season bringing a unique charm. Spring provides vibrant blooms, while autumn showcases golden hues. The dense forests offer shade during warmer months, and the tranquil environment is enjoyable in all seasons.

Q: Are there any running routes that follow river valleys?
A: Yes, the region around Almarza is characterized by river valleys, and several running routes incorporate these scenic features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40014075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Canto loop from El Torilejo&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading you through picturesque river valleys with gentle ascents.

Q: What can I expect regarding elevation on the running trails?
A: The trails around Almarza feature varied elevation profiles, from gentle ascents in river valleys to more significant climbs in hilly areas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064115251/castro-alto-de-la-cruz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Alto de la Cruz Loop&lt;/a&gt; includes an elevation gain of over 250 meters, providing a good workout for those seeking inclines.
tips: Near the car park of the Acebal de Garagüeta Natural Reserve, there is this great viewpoint where we will find an information panel on Acebal.
Walking along the Senda del Acebal, within the Acebal de Garagüeta Nature Reserve, we come across this beautiful hut that will come in handy in case we want to take shelter.
Ready to discover one of the most beautiful natural areas of Soria? In the Acebal de Garagüeta nature reserve we will find a large number of hollies together, apart from great views at all times.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint of El Acebal de Garagüeta
